confirmed.note: compile_split() is a copy of compile(): identical entry-module/entry-func lookup loop, verbatim 5-line std_memory linear-memory preamble block, same namespace-open/forward-decls/function-partition scaffolding — split flag bolted on. Same commit also duplicates the emit_struct method-emission body into split vs non-split branches.

confirmed.where: cpp/engine/src/engine.cpp:946-967 (setup_method_ref_info lambda body) vs :1583-1601 (inline pending_ref_info_ setup)
confirmed.note: The PendingRefInfo population block (info.caller_scope=scope; loop over message_creation().fields(); Reference check; scope->has + is_list/is_map -> field_to_var; has_refs guard) exists verbatim twice — once factored into a lambda, once left inline. Author extracted the lambda but did not replace the original inline copy.

false_positives.what_happened: These .snapshot.cpp files are emitted by the ball C++ compiler itself; every snapshot embeds the same fixed runtime preamble (includes block + BallDyn helpers + inline ball_map_copy/entries/keys/values). The within-file 'duplicate' the detector found is that repeated emitted preamble appearing once near the top and again where a second program/module is concatenated. line scores were low (0.05-0.43) which already signals weak text overlap on a boilerplate runtime header.
false_positives.fix_proposal: Apply #065 generated-skip BEFORE the dedup pass: (a) match a generated-code marker in the first ~3 lines — extend the default marker set (protobuf/moc/ggml) with a configurable regex and ship '// Generated by ' as a default prefix; (b) add a path-glob exclude default for **/snapshots/** and **/__snapshots__/**. Either filter drops all 10 hits.

false_positives.what_happened: Two emit_* helpers in the codegen visitor share the same boilerplate skeleton (emit_indent / out_ << ... / read_meta(func) / sanitize_name(func.name()) / map_return_type prologue) that recurs across nearly every emit_function* method in this compiler. The detector keys on the shared prologue idiom of a code-emitter class, not on a copied algorithm.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: raise the min-distinct-token / min-significant-line floor so that blocks dominated by a recurring per-method prologue (same N leading lines shared by 3+ siblings in one file) are demoted; or compute an in-file 'idiom frequency' — a chunk whose head k lines match >=3 other chunk heads in the same file is boilerplate, not a clone. This avoids flagging emitter-method prologues.

cycle.evidence: archcheck --graph on repo root: nodes=72 edges=32 sccs_cyclic=0 largest_scc=1. C++ surface is small (34 non-snapshot .cpp/.h, dirs cpp/ tests/ examples/) — project is mostly Dart/TS with a self-hosted C++ engine; include graph is a DAG, no closing back-edge exists.
